articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.headline: Dortmund captain Emre Can out for season with torn ACL
articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

images.name: Can Dortmund torn ACL_260228 [1296x729]
images.caption: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.
images.alt: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.

images.name: Bayern Munich wins 3-2 thriller over Dortmund
images.caption: Bayern Munich gets a late goal from Joshua Kimmich to beat Borussia Dortmund 3-2 in Der Klassiker.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  FC Bayern München 1, Eintracht Frankfurt 0. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Konrad Laimer   following a fast break.
Goal!  FC Bayern München 1, Eintracht Frankfurt 1. Hugo Ekitike (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Willian Pacho.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Aleksandar Pavlovic replaces Konrad Laimer because of an injury.
First Half ends, FC Bayern München 1, Eintracht Frankfurt 1.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Kim Min-Jae replaces Matthijs de Ligt.
Second Half begins FC Bayern München 1, Eintracht Frankfurt 1.
Robin Koch (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  FC Bayern München 2, Eintracht Frankfurt 1. Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Ellyes Skhiri (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card.
Mario Götze (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Alphonso Davies replaces Noussair Mazraoui.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Mathys Tel replaces Joshua Kimmich.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Niels Nkounkou replaces Junior Dina Ebimbe.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Jean-Mattéo Bahoya replaces Hugo Ekitike.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Donny van de Beek replaces Aurélio Buta.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Hugo Larsson replaces Mario Götze.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Lovro Zvonarek replaces Raphaël Guerreiro.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Timothy Chandler replaces Ellyes Skhiri.

article.headline: Kane double steers Bayern to 2-1 win over Frankfurt
article.description: Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane scored once in either half to guide them to a 2-1 Bundesliga victory over Eintracht Frankfurt on Saturday
article.linkText: Kane double steers Bayern to win vs. Frankfurt

images.name: Harry Kane's brace guides Bayern past Frankfurt
images.caption: Harry Kane becomes the third player in Bundesliga history to score 35 goals in a single season after a 2-1 win.

article.story:
Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane scored once in either half to guide his side to a 2-1 Bundesliga victory over Eintracht Frankfurt on Saturday in a dress rehearsal for Tuesday's Champions League semifinal first leg against Real Madrid.

The Bavarians, who saw Bayer Leverkusen end their 11-year-Bundesliga reign by securing the league crown two weeks ago, have only the Champions League trophy left to fight for with departing coach Thomas Tuchel eager to leave on a high note.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

They went in front when Kane tapped in a Konrad Laimer assist after the Austrian's fine run in the ninth minute. But Laimer had to be taken off injured a little later.

Eintracht levelled in the 23rd with Hugo Ekitike's shot from 18 metres but Kane restored order with a penalty just past the hour for his 35th league goal.

It was his 400th career goal at senior level for clubs and country, and his 42nd goal this season across all competitions was also a personal best for the England captain.

The hosts also had to take off defender Matthijs de Ligt at halftime after he suffered some rough challenges.

"Ahead of a key Champions League game such matches are not always easy. We then lost Konrad and Matthijs," Tuchel told a press conference. "But a win is the best preparation. Now we can fully focus on Tuesday."

He will, however, need to wait to see if De Ligt and Laimer will recover in time.

"De Ligt has pain in the knee ligaments and Konrad has an ankle injury. We now will have to wait and see what the X-rays show and hope they can make it for Tuesday."

Bayern remain in second place with three games left to play on 69 points, 12 behind Leverkusen, who needed a last-gasp equaliser to rescue a 2-2 draw against third-placed VfB Stuttgart, who have 64.

Fellow Champions League semifinalists Borussia Dortmund, who host Paris Saint-Germain on Wednesday, suffered a 4-1 loss to direct rivals RB Leipzig who held on to fourth place.

Leipzig are on 62 points, five ahead of fifth-placed Dortmund, with the top four finishers guaranteed a place in next season's Champions League group stage.

Germany is likely to earn a fifth spot as one of the top two in UEFA's coefficient table depending on the remaining results in European competition this season.
